headlines.title: UK companies linked to payments for small boat crossings, BBC finds
headlines.description: Smugglers are directing migrants to pay using a network of British businesses, secret filming reveals.

headlines.title: Bodies of missing Italian divers found in Maldives
headlines.description: The bodies of all five Italian divers who died in the Maldives last week have now been found, officials tell the BBC.

headlines.title: Are baby boomers like me the luckiest generation in history?
headlines.description: As young graduates criticise the terms of their student debt, Evan Davis asks whether baby boomers really were the luckiest generation

headlines.title: UK growth forecast upgraded by IMF but risks remain
headlines.description: Growth has been upgraded from 0.8% to 1% for 2026 in the influential body's latest forecast.

headlines.title: Parents could face bigger fines for child's crimes under youth justice shake-up
headlines.description: In the most extreme cases, parents could face jail if they fail to take action to address their child's behaviour.

headlines.title: At least six Americans exposed to Ebola during DR Congo outbreak
headlines.description: One of the six Americans believed to have been exposed is experiencing symptoms, according to media reports.

headlines.title: Rai first Englishman to win US PGA Championship since 1919
headlines.description: England's Aaron Rai wins the first major title of his career with a magnificent final-round 65 in the US PGA Championship at Aronimink.

headlines.title: New BBC boss warns 'tough choices are unavoidable' as he starts role
headlines.description: Ex-Google executive Matt Brittin says the BBC has "never been more needed" but faces "very real challenges".

headlines.title: 'All I thought about was running for my life': Footballer recalls train stabbing attack
headlines.description: Footballer Jonathan Gjoshe was among the 11 passengers seriously injured in a knife attack on a train as it travelled through Cambridgeshire last November.

headlines.title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak docks in Rotterdam
headlines.description: The ship's crew are expected to self-isolate after arriving at the Dutch port, their final destination.

headlines.title: Israeli forces boarding Gaza-bound flotilla near Cyprus, activists say
headlines.description: Live broadcasts from the Global Sumud flotilla show commandos boarding several boats trying to breach the Gaza maritime blockade.

headlines.title: 'Calm, humble, one of the nicest people you'll meet': Childhood coach on golf superstar Rai
headlines.description: Darren Prosser says he is lost for words after the golf star makes history and wins the US PGA.

headlines.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
headlines.description: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on  28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.

headlines.title: Watch: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
headlines.description: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

headlines.title: Inside the 'kill-zone' on Ukraine's front line, where new weapons have transformed war
headlines.description: Machines are increasingly replacing humans on the front line, but troops are still vital for defending territory.

headlines.title: She escaped child marriage ‘to a pervert’. Now she’s a divisive first lady
headlines.description: The private world of one of Africa's most powerful political women is revealed in a BBC World Service exclusive.

headlines.title: HS2 failings blamed on high-speed focus and political pressure
headlines.description: A new reviews confirms the high-speed rail line's "original sins" include a technical design, changing political priorities and ballooning costs.

headlines.title: Tube strike set to go ahead after failed talks
headlines.description: Another wave of industrial action is due to take place on London Underground from 12:00 on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Escaped tiger shot by German police after attacking man
headlines.description: The tiger escaped a facility owned by local trainer Carmen Zander, also known as Germany's "Tiger Queen", according to local media reports.

headlines.title: Instant AI answers could undermine people's intelligence, warns Royal Observatory
headlines.description: Paddy Rodgers said the Observatory's rich history showed the power of human knowledge and the need to avoid "dependence" on AI.

headlines.title: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls
headlines.description: On Monday Iran said it had responded to the latest US proposal and that exchanges with Washington were continuing.

headlines.title: More than 100 maternity staff sue NHS over gas exposure
headlines.description: Staff say they suffered from symptoms including fatigue, anxiety, headaches and "brain fog".

headlines.title: Waste carrier licences to be tightened as part of illegal dumping crackdown
headlines.description: The proposed changes come after a cow named Beau Vine got approved for a waste removal licence.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Does Labour’s Leadership Race Mean A Battle Over Brexit?
headlines.description: Wes Streeting and Andy Burnham have said they want the UK to rejoin the EU.

headlines.title: Why Democrats are panicking about losing California
headlines.description: Has America’s largest state become ungovernable?

headlines.title: Why Rai winning his first major in Rocky's backyard feels fitting
headlines.description: England's Aaron Rai winning the US PGA Championship seals a fascinating journey to the summit of the golfing world.

headlines.title: West Ham relegation may cost London taxpayers £2.5m
headlines.description: London taxpayers may have to pay an extra £2.5m if West Ham are relegated from the Premier League, because of the club's lease agreement for London Stadium.

headlines.title: Giroud, Azpilicueta and McCarthy join BBC World Cup team
headlines.description: BBC Sport announces its plans for the 2026 Fifa World Cup next month including coverage of all 104 matches in the United States, Canada and Mexico.

headlines.title: England call up former 'Baby Bok' Janse van Rensburg
headlines.description: Former South Africa Under-20 centre Benhard Janse van Rensburg could make his England debut this summer.

headlines.title: Selfish Salah should be dropped from Anfield finale - Rooney
headlines.description: Mohamed Salah should be dropped from Liverpool's season finale for "selfish" criticism of the club, says Wayne Rooney.

headlines.title: England pass Six Nations stress test, but rivals will return
headlines.description: England have established a stranglehold on the women's Test game, but France, defeated in Sunday's Grand Slam decider, are potentially their most dangerous rival
